title: He sold his stake in a billion-dollar company for $50,000 #Shorts
desc: In 1992 two college friends founded Keurig and built the single-serve coffee pod that ended up in millions of kitchens and offices. But co-founder John Sylvan clashed with investors, was forced out in 1997, and sold his entire stake for just $50,000. Years later Keurig became part of an $18.7 billion deal — while his machines kept brewing, cup after cup.
